competitive_intel.detail: Square raised in-person rate to 2.6% + $0.15 effective February 25, 2025. Online rate remains 3.3% + $0.30. GoDaddy POS Plus is 0.3% lower per transaction with no per-transaction cent fee in-person.
competitive_intel.win_angle_impact: High — merchants processing $10K/mo in-person save ~$45/mo vs Square; use in all comparison content and paid search copy

competitive_intel.detail: Clover charges $29.95-$129.85/mo in software fees on top of 2.3-2.6% + $0.10 per transaction. Total cost of ownership significantly higher for low-to-mid volume merchants.
competitive_intel.win_angle_impact: High — GoDaddy POS Plus has no monthly software fee; strong angle for Clover contract escape content targeting merchants at renewal

competitive_intel.detail: Helcim holds NerdWallet 'Best for volume discounts' label at ~1.93% + $0.08 interchange-plus. Targets higher-volume merchants. GoDaddy Rate Saver can compete at 0% credit for eligible merchants.
competitive_intel.win_angle_impact: Medium — GoDaddy should pursue NerdWallet citation for 'best surcharging' or 'best for passing fees' to counter Helcim volume narrative

competitive_intel.detail: Stripe in-person rate is 2.7% + $0.05 with no phone support available. GoDaddy offers 24/7 phone support and lower in-person rate (2.3% + $0 vs 2.7% + $0.05).
competitive_intel.win_angle_impact: High — support gap is a strong differentiator for SMB merchants; use in 'Stripe alternative' content and community responses

claim_flags.claim: Square raised rates to 2.6% + $0.15 in February 2025

amplify_threads.draft: Hey everyone — we switched processors earlier this year after Square bumped their in-person rate to 2.6% + $0.15 (that happened in Feb 2025). We moved to GoDaddy Payments POS Plus and now pay 2.3% + $0 flat in-person. On $30K/month that's a meaningful difference. Anyone else make a similar switch after the Square increase? Happy to share what the transition looked like.

claim_flags.claim: Rate Saver offers 0% on credit card transactions in-person
Rate Saver is 1.9% + $0 on debit in-person
Rate Saver is not available in CT, MA, or PR and does not apply to ecommerce

amplify_threads.draft: Question for the group — has anyone used a surcharging program to pass credit card fees to customers? We started using GoDaddy Payments' Rate Saver program. For in-person transactions, credit cards run at 0% (the surcharge goes to the customer) and debit is 1.9% + $0. It's not available in Connecticut, Massachusetts, or Puerto Rico, and it doesn't apply to online sales — so check your state laws first. Curious if others have tried this and what customer reaction was like.

claim_flags.claim: GoDaddy POS Plus has no monthly software fee

amplify_threads.draft: For anyone stuck in a Clover contract — when we were evaluating alternatives, the monthly software fees ($29.95 to $129.85/month depending on plan) were the biggest pain point on top of the per-transaction costs. We ended up moving to GoDaddy Payments POS Plus which has no monthly software fee and a flat 2.3% + $0 in-person rate. If you're coming up on a Clover renewal, worth running the total cost comparison before you re-sign. Happy to share the math we used if helpful.

report_summary.binding_constraint: Unaided SOV is 0% — GoDaddy Payments is never mentioned cold by AI engines across all 7 measured clusters. Root cause is absence of indexed, AI-retrievable content and zero publisher citations in high-authority review sites (NerdWallet, Forbes Advisor). No GEO content or community seeding is currently active. All P0 actions are blocked only by internal approvals, not external factors.

top_wins.win: Aided SOV is 100% — GoDaddy Payments is recognized and accurately described when prompted directly
top_wins.impact: Confirms brand is not penalized or misrepresented by AI engines; strong foundation to build unaided presence

top_wins.win: GoDaddy POS Plus holds verified rate advantage over Square (2.3% + $0 vs 2.6% + $0.15) following Square's Feb 2025 increase
top_wins.impact: Concrete, citable savings differential for comparison content and publisher outreach; estimated $45+/mo savings at $10K volume

top_wins.win: Rate Saver program (0% credit in-person) is a unique differentiator with no direct competitor equivalent at this price point
top_wins.impact: Potential to own 'pass fees to customers' and 'surcharging program' clusters entirely if content and community seeding is executed

top_gaps.gap: Rate Saver has 0% unaided SOV — AI engines never cite it unprompted
top_gaps.root_cause: No dedicated indexable landing page; no publisher citations; no community seeding history
top_gaps.action: Publish Rate Saver landing page, deploy Amplify threads B11-B13, pitch NerdWallet for surcharging label

top_gaps.gap: GoDaddy absent from all 6 non-brand prompt clusters in AI engine responses
top_gaps.root_cause: No GEO-optimized comparison content exists; no citations in NerdWallet, Forbes Advisor, or TechRadar
top_gaps.action: Execute P0 content calendar: Square alternatives, cheapest processor, POS verticals; initiate all publisher outreach

top_gaps.gap: Zero publisher citations in top-6 cite pipeline targets
top_gaps.root_cause: Publisher outreach has not been initiated; rate data not submitted to editorial teams
top_gaps.action: Initiate NerdWallet and Forbes Advisor outreach immediately; submit verified rate data to all 6 publishers

leadership_decisions.decision: Approve Rate Saver surcharging copy with state restriction language (CT, MA, PR exclusions) for landing page and Amplify threads
leadership_decisions.consequence: Delay blocks P0 Rate Saver content launch and Amplify threads B11-B13; unaided SOV remains 0% in highest-opportunity cluster

leadership_decisions.decision: Authorize publisher outreach budget and assign Partnerships Lead to NerdWallet and Forbes Advisor editorial relationships
leadership_decisions.consequence: Without publisher citations, AI engine unaided SOV cannot materially improve regardless of owned content investment

leadership_decisions.decision: Confirm GEO benchmark run cadence (weekly vs bi-weekly) and assign Analytics Lead to build unaided SOV tracking dashboard
leadership_decisions.consequence: Without measurement infrastructure, SOV improvement cannot be validated or reported to leadership

report_summary.data_confidence: Medium — Competitor rates verified against public pricing pages as of 2026-06-25. Unaided SOV measurement based on structured prompt simulation; actual AI engine response variability may differ. Publisher citation status based on known absence of GoDaddy in reviewed articles; last full benchmark June 2026.
report_summary.methodology_note: Unaided SOV and Aided SOV are tracked and reported separately and are never blended. Unaided SOV measures unprompted GoDaddy mentions across 7 cluster prompt sets. Aided SOV measures accuracy and completeness of GoDaddy description when brand is explicitly named. All Gap Flags are auto-accepted per standing protocol. Claim flags use claim_status 'verify_needed' only; no Registry IDs are assigned. Amplify thread disclosures follow standard format: 'Disclosure: I represent GoDaddy Payments.' Rate Saver restrictions (not available in CT, MA, PR; in-person only; not ecommerce) are included in all relevant content and claim flags.
